problem: Every PlayActivity in Arbor applies the same surface to every child at the same age band, regardless of what the child is absorbed in. A 3-year-old who loves trains and a 3-year-old who loves dinosaurs receive identical cards. The parent either skips the activity (not into this right now) or runs it perfunctorily. The developmental work is sound; the framing is generic. Kinedu, Lovevery, and HOMER have no cross-domain interest record. Arbor's longitudinal data uniquely enables this. Two buildable layers: (1) interests[] field on ChildProfile, safe and additive; (2) interest-boost scoring in select.ts rankDailyPlay, deterministic and LLM-free as the default path. The LLM theme-rewrite is a guarded follow-up gated on arbor-ai cost review per CIL para 2.
primaryUser: Parent of a 1-6 year old, 3-14 days post-activation (the primary Daily Play cohort). Already using the Daily Play card on Overview. Recognises the activity is broadly right for the age but feels it does not reflect who their child actually is today. Secondary user: the returning D30+ parent who sees the child's interest record evolve over time (trains at age 2, dinosaurs at age 4) -- the longitudinal signal that differentiates Arbor from every competitor.
ia:
CI-29 is additive within two existing surfaces. No new tab, route, or ActiveTab value is introduced.

SURFACE A -- ProfileEditDrawer: A new Interests section is inserted between Languages and Strengths in the drawer scroll. This is the sole collection point. Chip-toggle pattern: 12 curated suggestion chips plus a plain-text add input. Parent-facing register throughout (kit.tsx idioms, index.css tokens; no PlayKit).

SURFACE B -- DailyPlayCard: When the selected activity has themeableContextSlot=true AND at least one interest is recorded, a passive interest-match badge appears on the card and the why-line references the matched interest. The badge is decorative and informational; no interaction required.

SURFACE C -- ChildProfile display section (components/sections/ChildProfile.tsx): The who-is-child SectionCard (Chapter 1) gains an Interests sub-field alongside Languages and School context, showing the first 3 recorded interests as Chip components (tone=lav) with an Add link that opens ProfileEditDrawer.

NO new tab, no new ActiveTab value, no new route. The DailyPlayTab (existing daily-play tab) passes interests down to the selector via the existing PlaySelectContext extension.
uxFlow:
FLOW 1 -- First-time interest collection (D1-D3 parent):
1. Parent opens ProfileEditDrawer via the Edit Profile button (avatar tap in Shell / ChildProfile page).
2. Drawer opens (slide-in from inline-end / left in RTL). Scrolls down to the new Interests section, which is positioned between Languages and Strengths.
3. Interests section shows: label 'What is [name] really into right now?' (13px muted uppercase), 12 suggestion chips in a wrapping flex row, and a small + Add your own text input below the chips.
4. Parent taps a chip (e.g. Trains). Chip transitions to active style (green-soft bg, green-ink text, green border, checkmark icon prepended). No toast, no confirmation -- immediate toggle.
5. Parent taps a second chip (e.g. Dinosaurs). Both now active.
6. Parent types 'Lego' in the Add input, presses Enter or taps the + button. A new chip appears in active state appended after the suggestions.
7. Parent taps Save changes. interests[] is persisted to ChildProfile (Firestore if authenticated, localStorage otherwise via existing ProfileContext.updateChild pattern).
8. Drawer closes. No forced app reload.

FLOW 2 -- Interest-boosted Daily Play card (same or next day):
1. Parent opens Today (Overview tab). DailyPlayCard renders as before.
2. If the selected activity has themeableContextSlot=true and interests[] is non-empty: a small Chip component (tone=lav, 11px) reads 'Matched to [child]'s love of [interest]' and appears directly below the title, replacing the existing stage-match reason chip.
3. The whatItBuilds line and all steps render exactly as before -- the developmental content is unchanged.
4. The why-line at the card bottom reads: 'Chosen because [name] loves [interest] -- and because it fits this stage.' (concern-match variant: 'Chosen because [name] loves [interest] -- and it matches what you have been working on.')
5. If the activity does not have themeableContextSlot=true, or interests[] is empty, the card renders exactly as today with no visual change.

FLOW 3 -- Interest update (returning parent):
1. Parent re-opens ProfileEditDrawer.
2. Interests section shows previously saved interests in active state. Parent can toggle off (deactivate) or add new ones.
3. A subtle timestamp line beneath the section reads 'Last updated [relative date]' (faint, 11px) if interests have been set before -- surfaces the longitudinal aspect without making it heavy.
4. Save persists the updated array. Next Daily Play pick re-scores with the updated interests.
uiSpec:
INTERESTS SECTION IN ProfileEditDrawer:
- Position: after the Languages field, before the Strengths textarea.
- Section label: text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wide, color var(--arbor-muted). Copy: 'What is [name] really into right now?'
- Helper text (below label, 11px, var(--arbor-faint)): 'Arbor uses this to frame activities in things [name] already loves.'
- Chip row: flex flex-wrap gap-2 mt-2. Each suggestion chip is a <button> with rounded-full px-3 py-1.5 text-[12px] font-bold transition. Inactive: bg var(--arbor-paper-deep), border 1px solid var(--arbor-rule-strong), color var(--arbor-muted). Active: bg var(--arbor-green-soft), border 1px solid rgba(52,178,119,0.40), color var(--arbor-green-ink), prepends a checkmark icon (lucide Check, w-3 h-3). Min touch target 44px via padding compensation on small chips.
- 12 suggested interests (EN): Trains, Dinosaurs, Animals, Trucks, Superheroes, Princesses, Cooking, Music, Water, Space, Building, Nature. HE equivalents provided to i18n.ts (see dataModel).
- Free-text add row: a small input (w-full rounded-xl px-3 py-2 text-xs, same inputStyle as other fields in the drawer) with placeholder 'Add your own...' (HE: ...) and a trailing + button (inline-flex items-center gap-1, color var(--arbor-green-ink)). On Enter or + tap: trim, deduplicate, append as new active chip. Max 20 interests total (silently ignore adds beyond 20; no error toast -- the visual chip count communicates the limit).
- Custom chips (user-added) render the same as suggestion chips in active state, with an X dismiss icon on the inline-end that removes them.
- Last-updated line: text-[11px] color var(--arbor-faint), renders only if interests[] was previously non-empty on drawer open. Copy: 'Last updated [timeago]'.
- Section is placed inside the existing space-y-4 div, same pattern as other fields.

INTEREST BADGE ON DailyPlayCard:
- A single Chip component (from kit.tsx, tone='lav') rendered immediately below the activity h2 title, before the whatItBuilds line.
- Copy: 'Matched to [name]'s love of [interest]' where [interest] is interests[0] (the first recorded interest -- no random selection, deterministic for stability within a day).
- Renders ONLY when: (a) activity.themeableContextSlot === true AND (b) childProfile.interests.length > 0.
- If neither condition is met, nothing renders -- the card is identical to today.
- The why-line (12px, var(--arbor-faint), bottom of card) is updated: when interest-matched, appends '-- and because [name] loves [interest]' to the existing concern-match or stage-match line.

INTEREST FIELD IN ChildProfile display (sections/ChildProfile.tsx):
- Inside Chapter 1 SectionCard (who-is-child, tone=mint), a new Field-like row labelled 'Interests' renders after Languages.
- Value: first 3 interests as Chip components (tone=lav, 11px), then if more exist a +N muted label.
- If interests[] is empty: a JumpLink-style inline button 'Add interests' that calls setActiveTab implicitly OR opens ProfileEditDrawer directly (the drawer already has an open prop; the ChildProfile page can lift the drawer state).

RTL / HEBREW:
- ProfileEditDrawer already uses dir=rtl-inherited layout (flex/grid auto-mirror under html[dir=rtl] from index.css).
- Chip row wraps correctly in RTL; the + Add input field's placeholder and button swap inline-end to inline-start.
- The checkmark and X icons auto-flip with the text direction.
- The suggestion chips use i18n.t() keys (see dataModel section for key list).
- The interest badge on DailyPlayCard: 'Matched to [name]'s love of [interest]' has a HE translation key.

A11Y:
- Each chip is a <button> with aria-pressed={isActive} and aria-label='[interest name], [active/not active]'.
- The custom add input has aria-label='Add a custom interest'.
- The chip row has role='group' aria-label='Interests'.
- Focus is not trapped in the chip section; Tab moves through chips in DOM order.
- The interest badge Chip on DailyPlayCard is presentational (no interaction); aria-hidden is acceptable if the why-line already conveys the information.
- Reduced-motion: the chip toggle has no animation beyond the style change (no transition on background for users who prefer-reduced-motion).

MOBILE-FIRST:
- ProfileEditDrawer is full-width on mobile (max-w-md, 100% below md breakpoint -- same as today).
- Chip row wraps naturally; chips are sized to comfortably wrap at 320px viewport width.
- DailyPlayCard interest badge is a single-line chip that wraps if needed; the card layout does not break.
- Touch targets: all chips >= 44px effective touch area (padding compensation).
statesEmptyLoadingError:
INTERESTS SECTION STATES:

Empty (no interests set, first open):
- All 12 suggestion chips shown in inactive state.
- Helper text visible.
- No last-updated line.
- Free-text input is empty and unfocused.

Partially filled (some chips active, drawer re-opened):
- Active chips show in active style.
- Last-updated line shows relative date.
- Custom chips appear at the end of the chip row with X dismiss icon.

Save loading:
- The Save changes button shows 'Saving...' and disabled state (opacity-60, same as today -- existing saving state in ProfileEditDrawer).
- Chip row remains interactive during save (consistent with other fields in the drawer which remain live).

Save error:
- The existing toast('Could not save changes', 'error') pattern from ProfileContext is sufficient. No special interests-specific error state.

Max interests reached (>20):
- Free-text add input shows placeholder 'Max 20 interests reached' (11px, var(--arbor-faint)). The + button is hidden (display:none). No toast -- the placeholder communicates the state.

DailyPlayCard STATES:

No interests recorded (interests[] empty):
- Card renders exactly as today. No badge, no modified why-line. No 'add interests' prompt on the card itself (the nudge lives in ChildProfile display, not in the daily card).

Interests recorded, activity NOT themeable (themeableContextSlot=false):
- Card renders exactly as today. No badge. The selector may have scored this activity higher due to the interest-boost in rankDailyPlay, but the card does not surface that internal scoring.

Interests recorded, activity IS themeable (themeableContextSlot=true):
- Interest badge renders below title.
- Why-line updated.
- All other card content (whatItBuilds, items, steps, actions) identical to today.

Loading (selector running on mount, existing skeleton state in OverviewTab):
- The existing Skeleton component renders for the entire DailyPlayCard section during load -- no change needed to the loading pattern.

ChildProfile display (Chapter 1 Interests field):

Empty:
- Renders 'Add interests' as a JumpLink-style inline button (color var(--arbor-green-ink)), which opens ProfileEditDrawer.

Filled:
- Renders up to 3 Chip components (tone=lav) then +N if more.
- No loading state (interests[] is always synchronously available from childProfile in ArborContext).
inScope: interests[] field added to ChildProfile type in app/src/types.ts (optional, string[], default empty array for backward compat)
interests[] persisted via existing ProfileContext.updateChild path (Firestore + localStorage fallback -- no new write path needed)

themeableContextSlot: boolean added to PlayActivity interface in app/src/playbank/content.ts -- marks activities whose object/context can be interest-substituted without changing developmental purpose
All existing PLAY_ACTIVITIES in content.ts annotated with themeableContextSlot: true/false (author decision per activity; default false is safe)

interestBoost scoring added to rankDailyPlay in app/src/playbank/select.ts: if activity.themeableContextSlot=true AND concerns[].length>0 (interest match proxy via free-text match), apply a 1.25x multiplier on top of existing bandScore * concernBoost * novelty. The interest scoring is additive, never replacing the existing concern/band logic
PlaySelectContext extended with interests?: string[] so the selector can receive the child's interest array from callers in OverviewTab and DailyPlayTab
Interest-match reason added to ScoredActivity.reason union: 'interest-match' alongside existing 'concern-match' and 'stage-match'
ProfileEditDrawer interests section: chip-toggle UI, 12 suggested interests, free-text add, max 20, save via existing updateChild, EN+HE i18n
DailyPlayCard interest badge: renders when themeableContextSlot=true and interests non-empty, using existing Chip from kit.tsx tone=lav
DailyPlayCard why-line updated to reference matched interest when interest-match reason is present
ChildProfile display (sections/ChildProfile.tsx) Chapter 1 Interests field: shows first 3 as lav Chips, empty state links to ProfileEditDrawer
i18n keys for all new copy in both EN and HE (app/src/lib/i18n.ts)
COPPA review on interests[] write path (arbor-safety gate -- new child-associated data field)
No clinical copy -- interests are a personalization input only, never a developmental signal surfaced to the parent
outOfScope: LLM theme-rewrite (full sentence substitution of activity text using the child's interest): gated on arbor-ai CIL para 2 cost review. The default implementation is pure template/scoring -- no LLM call in this build. LLM path is a fast-follow only after cost gate clears.
Interest-based developmental inference of any kind: no copy reads 'your child's interest in X suggests Y ability'. The interest record is a personalization input, not a diagnostic signal.

Interest field on the onboarding flow (AP-049 5-step onboarding): interests[] is collected in ProfileEditDrawer post-onboarding. Adding it to onboarding is a separate decision and would touch the onboarding flow which is currently Tier-C gated.

Interest history / longitudinal interest timeline display: the array is timestamped at the field level (updatedAt on ChildProfile), but a dedicated interest-history view is out of scope for this sprint. The longitudinal signal is preserved in the data model for CI-30.

themeableContextSlot on CI-27 Capability Graph nodes: the field is specified here on PlayActivity (existing) and should be carried forward into the Capability node schema when CI-27 is built. It is not CI-29's responsibility to define the full Capability type.
Interest personalization in any surface other than Daily Play (e.g., bedtime stories, hero journeys): the moat extends there but is out of scope for CI-29.
Any edit to index.css (locked; arbor-design owns). All styling uses existing token var() references.
successMetric: Primary: D14 Daily Play activity completion rate for interest-matched activities vs baseline (target +20pp). Tracked by existing analytics.ts track() call on the 'play.did' event -- add an 'interest_matched' boolean property to the payload. Secondary: interests[] completion rate among new profiles within D7 (target 60% of new profiles have at least one interest set within 7 days of account creation). Tertiary: D30 retention among parents who have set interests vs those who have not (directional, not a launch gate). Time-in-app is not a metric per backlog spec.
openClinicalQuestions: COPPA gate (arbor-safety, required before build-ready): interests[] is a new child-associated data field written to Firestore under the child's record. arbor-safety must confirm: (1) interests[] qualifies as personal data under COPPA-2026 (child-associated free-text input); (2) the existing COPPA consent framework (ConsentGrant in types.ts) covers this write path or a new ConsentPurpose is required; (3) the deletion path (eraseEverything in lib/childData.ts) already covers ChildProfile fields (it should, as it deletes the child document, but this must be confirmed); (4) the export path (exportChildData) includes interests[] in the JSON output (additive, should be automatic as it exports the ChildProfile).

Free-text interest content moderation (arbor-safety, scoped): parents can type any string as a custom interest. The field is parent-entered and displayed only to that parent (not shared, not model-input in Phase 1). In the LLM substitution follow-up, the interest string will be injected into a prompt -- at that point a content filter is required. For Phase 1 (score-boost only, no LLM), the risk is low: the interest string is never sent to a model and is only displayed to the parent who entered it. arbor-safety to confirm Phase 1 risk level is acceptable without a content filter.

Interest array as a longitudinal record -- data retention framing (arbor-clinical-lead, advisory): the backlog spec notes that the interest record evolves over time ('a 2-year-old who loved trains and a 4-year-old who pivoted to dinosaurs; the transition is itself a developmental signal the parent can observe'). Before any surface presents the interest history to the parent as a developmental narrative, arbor-clinical-lead must confirm the copy framing. For Phase 1, interests[] is a flat array with no history shown; this question is deferred to the longitudinal display feature (CI-30 or later).

LLM substitution cost gate (arbor-ai, required before enabling LLM path): the default Phase 1 implementation uses score-boosting only (no LLM call). Before enabling the LLM sentence-substitution path (fast-follow), arbor-ai must confirm per-call cost is within CIL para 2 quota and that the substitution prompt is constrained to surface-object substitution only (not capability description rewrite). The LLM path must also pass screenModelOutput on every substituted string with the capability's prohibitedDiagnosticClaims[] extension (CI-27 pattern).

Suggested interest chip list -- clinical neutrality check (arbor-clinical-lead, low priority): the 12 suggested chips (trains, dinosaurs, animals, trucks, superheroes, princesses, cooking, music, water, space, building, nature) are parent-facing labels for personalization, not developmental categories. No clinical review is technically required for a list of common child interests. However, if the list is expanded significantly or if future chips include sensory-specific items (e.g. 'slime', 'water table', 'spinning'), arbor-clinical-lead should confirm no chip label inadvertently reads as a sensory-processing signal (SPD territory per CI-33 guardrail).
